req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three key decision axes: (1) amount threshold ($5,000 boundary for manual verification), (2) idempotency on duplicate Idempotency-Key submission, and (3) MICR decode failure returning 422. I selected EP to cover a normal below-threshold deposit (happy path), NEG for invalid MICR image, BVA for the $5,000 exact boundary, DT for the idempotency duplicate-key scenario (crossing state), and STC for the state transition from initial submission to duplicate replay. Together these achieve full requirement coverage per R-FIN-094.

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_returns_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR is available
Cheque amount is $1,000 (well within the sub-$5,000 partition)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload, amount=1000, and unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body does NOT contain manual_review=true
A deposit_id is present in the response
trace: R-FIN-094: amounts below $5,000 do not require manual verification
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_returns_pending(
    auth_client,  # <fixture>: returns authenticated requests.Session
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture>: returns bytes of a MICR-valid cheque image
    api_base_url,  # <fixture>: returns base URL string
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = <build_multipart_payload>(valid_cheque_image, amount=1000)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_client.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = auth_client.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        files=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is not True
    assert "deposit_id" in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_invalid_micr_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A cheque image with corrupt/unreadable MICR line is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with the corrupt MICR image payload and a unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an error code referencing MICR decode failure
No deposit_id is present in the response
trace: R-FIN-094: error pathway - 422 when image fails MICR decode
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_invalid_micr_returns_422(
    auth_client,  # <fixture>
    corrupt_micr_image,  # <fixture>: returns bytes of a cheque image with bad MICR
    api_base_url,  # <fixture>
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = <build_multipart_payload>(corrupt_micr_image, amount=500)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_client.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = auth_client.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        files=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error", {}).get("code", "")
    assert "micr" in error_code.lower() or "decode" in error_code.lower()
    assert "deposit_id" not in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_exactly_5000_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR is available
Cheque amount is exactly $5,000 (the boundary value)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image, amount=5000, and unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains decisioning_queued=true or equivalent queue reference
trace: R-FIN-094: cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification
R-FIN-094: boundary at $5,000 triggers decisioning queue
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_exactly_5000_requires_manual_review(
    auth_client,  # <fixture>
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,  # <fixture>
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = <build_multipart_payload>(valid_cheque_image, amount=5000)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_client.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = auth_client.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        files=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True
    assert body.get("decisioning_queued") is True or body.get("queue_ref") is not None

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_idempotent_duplicate_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR is available
The same Idempotency-Key has already been used in a prior successful submission
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque (first call) with valid image, amount=2000, fixed Idempotency-Key
Record first response deposit_id and status
POST /deposits/mobile-cheque (second call) with identical Idempotency-Key and same payload
Compare second response to first response
expected: Second response deposit_id equals first response deposit_id
Second response status equals first response status
No duplicate deposit record is created
trace: R-FIN-094: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_idempotent_duplicate_key_returns_original_result(
    auth_client,  # <fixture>
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,  # <fixture>
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = <build_multipart_payload>(valid_cheque_image, amount=2000)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_client.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp1 = auth_client.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        files=payload, headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp1.status_code == 202
    body1 = resp1.json()
    resp2 = auth_client.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        files=<build_multipart_payload>(valid_cheque_image, amount=2000),
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 202
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body2["deposit_id"] == body1["deposit_id"]
    assert body2["status"] == body1["status"]

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_state_transitions_pending_to_queued
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR is available
Cheque amount is $6,000 (above $5,000 threshold to force decisioning queue)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image, amount=6000, unique Idempotency-Key
Assert initial response state is 'pending' and manual_review=true
GET /deposits/{deposit_id} after a short poll delay to observe state transition
Assert deposit record reflects decisioning_queued state
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 202 with status='pending' and manual_review=true
Subsequent GET returns status that is 'pending' or 'under_review' (decisioning queued)
State never regresses from a later state back to an earlier one
trace: R-FIN-094: decisioning queued as expected outcome
R-FIN-094: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification (state transition)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
import uuid

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_state_transitions_pending_to_queued(
    auth_client,  # <fixture>
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,  # <fixture>
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = <build_multipart_payload>(valid_cheque_image, amount=6000)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_client.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = auth_client.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        files=payload, headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True
    deposit_id = body["deposit_id"]
    time.sleep(2)  # allow async decisioning worker to enqueue
    poll = auth_client.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/{deposit_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_client.token}"},
    )
    assert poll.status_code == 200
    poll_status = poll.json()["status"]
    assert poll_status in ("pending", "under_review", "decisioning_queued")
